Hallucinations.text: Keonna Thomas, a 30-year-old woman from Philadelphia, was charged by the FBI for attempting to travel overseas to fight for ISIS. She was one of three women arrested that week on terror charges, alongside two New York women. Thomas, who used the aliases "Young Lioness" and "Fatayat Al Khilafah," expressed support for joining ISIS through social media messages. She purchased a visa to Turkey and a ticket to Barcelona, intending to travel to Syria. She faces charges of attempting to provide material support to a designated foreign terrorist organization and could be sentenced to up to 15 years in prison.

Blue Bell ice cream has temporarily shut down its Broken Arrow, Oklahoma, manufacturing plant due to the discovery of listeria contamination. This action follows public health warnings advising consumers not to consume any Blue Bell-branded products made at this specific plant. The warning does not affect other Blue Bell products made at different facilities. This is the third time Blue Bell has taken such action in response to listeria outbreaks. The company is investigating the cause of the contamination and has recalled certain products. The CDC advises checking freezers for recalled products and disposing of them. This marks the first product recall in Blue Bell's 108-year history.

Mary Kay Letourneau Fualaau and her husband Vili Fualaau, despite their controversial past, are still happily married and plan to celebrate their 10th wedding anniversary. Mary Kay intends to teach again and wants her status as a registered sex offender lifted, while Vili speaks about his struggles with alcoholism and depression. Kanye West settled a lawsuit with photographer Daniel Ramos, who had filed the suit after West assaulted him in 2013. As part of the settlement, West apologized to Ramos, and the two shook hands. The case was dismissed to the satisfaction of both parties, though the financial details of the settlement were not disclosed. This resolution follows West's previous legal actions related to similar incidents.

Blues legend B.B. King was hospitalized for dehydration caused by his Type II diabetes but was released and back home resting with his grandchildren. He thanked those who expressed concern for his health. King, 89, is known for his signature guitar named Lucille and has 30 Grammy nominations. He previously canceled a tour in 2012 due to dehydration and exhaustion after a show in Chicago.
Chris Copeland of the Indiana Pacers was stabbed outside a nightclub in New York,

two Atlanta Hawks players, Pero Antic and Thabo Sefolosha, were arrested on charges unrelated to the stabbing. The incident occurred near the Fulton Houses project, not inside or immediately around the club. Copeland suffered a knife wound to his left elbow and abdomen and is in stable condition. Antic and Sefolosha were arrested for obstructing governmental administration and disorderly conduct, with Sefolosha facing an additional charge of resisting arrest. The Hawks players were checking on their friend Copeland when they were arrested.

Selena's family is creating a digital version of the late singer that will be able to walk, talk, sing, and dance, using technology from Acrovirt LLC. This project, named "Selena the One," aims to continue her legacy and introduce her to new fans through new music and collaborations. The technology is set to debut in 2018.

The incident on Alaska Airlines Flight 448 involved a ramp agent from Menzies Aviation who fell asleep in the cargo hold of the plane. The plane made an emergency landing after the pilot heard banging from the cargo hold and suspected a person might be inside. The ramp agent was taken to a hospital as a precaution and later discharged after passing a drug test. Alaska Airlines is investigating the incident, and the employee had undergone a full criminal background check before being hired. The flight eventually arrived in Los Angeles with a delay.

Emy Afalava's situation highlights the ongoing struggle for full citizenship rights for American Samoans, despite their birth on U.S. soil and lifelong adherence to American law. This discrepancy is rooted in historical Supreme Court decisions that have applied racial discrimination to territories acquired later, such as American Samoa. The case now before the D.C. Circuit aims to resolve this injustice and align the status of American Samoans with the principles of the Fourteenth Amendment.
issue of citizenship rights. This situation underscores the need to address lingering racial biases in legal interpretations that affect citizenship and civil rights.

Iman and Siham, collectively known as Faarrow, are working on their debut album in Los Angeles, blending pop with hip-hop and African percussion. They have been collaborating with Elijah Kelley, an actor and talented music producer, who has helped them refine their sound. The sisters also work with the UNHCR, advocating for refugees and supporting projects that provide sustainable income for women in Kenya and Somalia through their socially conscious brand, "Wish Creatively."
and preparing for the release of their first single. They aim to inspire and represent their generation and Somalia through their creative endeavors.

Senate passed a Medicare reform bill with a broad bipartisan vote of 92 to 8, which includes a permanent solution to the "doc fix" and a two-year extension of a children's health insurance program. President Barack Obama is expected to sign the bill, which had already been approved by the House. This action prevents immediate cuts to physicians' payments that would have occurred due to the expiration of the last temporary "doc fix." .

Superman: Dawn of Justice" leaked online, featuring overlapping commentary about the superheroes and scenes of both Batman and Superman, including a face-off between the two. The trailer was scheduled to officially premiere in Imax theaters on Monday. The film, starring Ben Affleck as Batman and Henry Cavill as Superman, is set for release on March 25, 2016.

Park Service and the National Park Foundation host various events including night-time astronomy parties, daytime Revolutionary War programs, Earth Day celebrations, and Junior Ranger activities. On Saturday and Sunday, 128 fee-charging parks, such as Yellowstone and Yosemite, will be free of charge. For more information and to find park sites near you, visit FindYourPark.com or www.nationalparkweek.org. Share your experiences using the hashtag #FindYourPark.

The second robotic probe sent into the Fukushima nuclear plant captured images showing a strange green glow, though TEPCO noted that the green glow was not visible from other angles and the cause was unknown. The robot also detected lower radiation levels and temperature than expected, indicating effective cooling systems. This mission is part of ongoing efforts to gather necessary data for decommissioning the plant, which remains too dangerous for human workers to enter directly.

celebrating its 50th anniversary
, including Eric Church and Keith Urban's powerful opening performance and Garth Brooks' salute to the U.S. military. Taylor Swift's presence and vocal contributions were also noted as highlights.

Prince Jan Zylinski challenged Nigel Farage to a duel in London's Hyde Park over Farage's immigration policy, which Zylinski believes discriminates against Poles living in Britain. Farage declined the challenge, stating he does not intend to cross swords with the prince.

, Zylinski offered an alternative challenge of a duel with words in a TV studio before the elections. Farage's spokesman indicated that Farage would not accept the sword duel but did not comment on the verbal duel offer. Zylinski also posted a video highlighting positive contributions of Poles to Britain.

Seven people, including Illinois State University associate men's basketball coach Torrey Ward and deputy athletic director Aaron Leetch, died when their small plane crashed while returning from the NCAA tournament final. The aircraft, a Cessna 414, went down near Bloomington, McLean County, Illinois. The plane was coming back from the NCAA Final Four championship game in Indianapolis. Ward and Leetch were remembered for their contributions to the university's athletics department.

The testimony described the harrowing experiences of survivors and family members affected by the Boston Marathon bombing, including Steve Woolfenden and his son Leo, Heather Abbott, Marc Fucarile, and the tragic death of Martin Richard. The prosecution aimed to portray Dzhokhar Tsarnaev as cold-hearted and remorseless, while the defense planned to present a softer portrayal of Tsarnaev, focusing on his upbringing. The jury must decide whether Tsarnaev will receive the death penalty or life in prison. This period of testimony concluded with the prosecution's case on Thursday, and the defense was expected to begin its case on Monday.

Nick Loeb is suing Sofia Vergara to prevent her from destroying their two frozen embryos conceived through in vitro fertilization. The lawsuit, filed anonymously, seeks to protect the embryos from being destroyed until both parties reach an agreement. Loeb wants to implant the embryos in a surrogate and bring them to term, while Vergara reportedly wants to destroy them. The case highlights the legal and ethical complexities surrounding embryo rights and ownership.

Hillary Clinton's campaign launch aimed to rebrand and reframe her image, focusing on a more empathetic and forward-looking approach. Despite criticism from some media outlets, her strategy included a video, new logo, and a road trip to connect with voters on a more personal level. The goal was to shift her image from one of self-absorption to one of empathy and support for everyday Americans. This marketing effort seeks to change not just how voters think about her, but how they feel about her as well. For Hillary's campaign to succeed, consistency in messaging and behavior will be crucial.

Sissa Abu Dahou, a grandmother of two, has dressed as a man for 43 years to support herself and her daughter in the conservative environment of Luxor, Egypt. After her husband's death when she was 21 and six months pregnant, she faced a patriarchal society that made it nearly impossible for women to earn a living. She worked as a brick maker and later as a shoe shiner, eventually gaining recognition and praise for her resilience and dedication to her family. Her story
. Her efforts were acknowledged by Egypt's President, who honored her as one of Egypt's ideal mothers.

These factors include the heinous, cruel, and depraved nature of the crime, the substantial planning and premeditation involved, the betrayal of the United States, and the lack of remorse displayed by Tsarnaev. The defense, however, argues that Tsarnaev was influenced by his older brother and that his brain had not fully developed, potentially mitigating his responsibility. The author
but due to the requirement of a unanimous verdict, any division among jurors could result in a life sentence instead.

The jury members, after more than 35 hours of deliberation over seven days, found former NFL star Aaron Hernandez guilty of first-degree murder in the 2013 shooting death of Odin Lloyd. They based their decision on the testimony of over 130 witnesses and the review of more than 400 pieces of evidence. The jurors emphasized the emotional toll of the trial and the seriousness of their task, contrasting it with the portrayal of trials in television shows. They highlighted the indifference shown by Hernandez, particularly his actions after the shooting, as a significant factor in their decision. The jurors also expressed their focus on the evidence rather than Hernandez's status as a former football player. Their verdict concluded a trial that had consumed their lives since late January.

Larry Johnson and other passengers on SkyWest Airlines Flight 5622 experienced fear and helplessness during an emergency landing in Buffalo, New York, after the plane descended rapidly due to what passengers perceived as a pressurization issue. The Federal Aviation Administration initially reported a pressurization problem but later did not reference this in a statement

No pressurization issues were found by maintenance personnel, and the National Transportation Safety Board is gathering information to understand the incident better. Fifteen passengers were examined at the airport, but no one was hospitalized. The flight was originally scheduled to fly from Chicago to Hartford, Connecticut.

Aaron Hernandez's arrogance and lack of remorse during his trial did not affect the verdict, which was based on the substantial evidence presented. The jury found him guilty of first-degree murder in the 2013 slaying of Odin Lloyd, along with unlawful possession of a firearm and ammunition. This decision was seen as the right one, despite the personal tragedy for both Lloyd and Hernandez. The NFL and the New England Patriots handled the situation appropriately, reflecting a more responsible approach compared to past incidents. Hernandez's case highlights the unpredictable nature of human behavior, even among those with promising futures.

Louis Jordan, 37, was rescued after being lost at sea for 66 days. He had been drifting on a 35-foot Pearson sailboat since a storm capsized it and broke his mast during a fishing trip off the coast of South Carolina. Despite his ordeal, Jordan was in surprisingly good condition when found by the crew of the Houston Express. He managed to survive by rigging a makeshift mast and sail, collecting fresh water, and catching fish that were attracted to his laundry. Jordan was taken to Sentara Norfolk General Hospital and was reported to be in good physical and mental condition, considering his circumstances. His father, Frank Jordan, expressed relief and pride in his son's resilience and faith.

The French prosecutor leading the investigation into the crash of Germanwings Flight 9525 stated that he was not aware of any video footage from on board the plane and urged anyone with such footage to immediately give it to the investigators. This statement came after claims by German daily Bild and French Paris Match of a cell phone video showing the final moments of the flight. However, officials from France's accident investigation agency and the French Gendarmerie denied the existence of such a video, stating that the reports were "completely wrong" and "unwarranted."

Robert Lewis Burns Jr., the original drummer of Lynyrd Skynyrd, died in a car crash on Friday night in Cartersville, Georgia. He was 64 years old. Burns was part of the band's original lineup that formed in Jacksonville, Florida, in 1965, but left before the release of their third album in 1975.

A nuclear submarine under repair at the Zvyozdochka shipyard in northwestern Russia caught fire due to insulation burning during welding work. The nuclear fuel had been removed from the reactor, and there were no armaments or hazardous materials on board
All personnel had evacuated safely, and the fire did not pose a threat to people or the shipyard.

The California Public Utilities Commission ordered Pacific Gas & Electric Co. to pay a record $1.6 billion penalty for unsafe operation of its gas transmission system, including the pipeline rupture that killed eight people in San Bruno in September 2010.

Hip-hop star Nelly was arrested on drug charges in Tennessee after a state trooper pulled over the private bus he was traveling in because it lacked required stickers. The arrest led to charges including felony possession of drugs, simple possession of marijuana, and possession of drug paraphernalia. Authorities found methamphetamine, marijuana, and drug paraphernalia during the search of the bus, along with several handguns. Nelly posted bond and was released from jail.

John Travolta, a prominent member of the Church of Scientology, has expressed his disinterest in HBO's new documentary "Going Clear: Scientology and the Prison of Belief," stating he has not seen it and does not wish to. He believes the documentary is based on negative experiences of disgruntled individuals and emphasizes his positive 40-year experience with Scientology, which he credits with helping him cope with the loss of his son. Travolta considers the church "brilliant" and supportive.

The crash involved a Lamborghini during a fantasy sports car ride at Walt Disney World Speedway, resulting in the death of the passenger, Gary Terry, and minor injuries to the driver, Tavon Watson. Petty Holdings, which runs the Exotic Driving Experience, expressed deep condolences to those affected by the tragedy.

The gastrointestinal illness has affected 100 people on the Celebrity Infinity, with 95 passengers and 5 crew members experiencing symptoms such as vomiting and diarrhea. The CDC is investigating the cause and will meet the ship in San Diego on Monday

Celebrity Cruises has increased cleaning procedures and is keeping passengers informed while collecting samples for testing. This is the third outbreak on the Celebrity Infinity since its construction in 2001, with previous incidents in 2006 and 2013.

Jia Jiang embarked on a project called "100 Days of Rejection" after quitting his job to pursue entrepreneurship, aiming to overcome his fear of rejection. This led to his book, "Rejection Proof," which offers tips on how to turn rejection into opportunity. His advice includes understanding that the fear of rejection often holds people back more than rejection itself, and that rejection is not a measure of an idea's merit. Jiang encourages people to ask why they were rejected and to find solutions, to set a number of rejections they can handle, and to build resilience against rejection. The Ebola epidemic has significantly impacted over 25,000 people, with more than 10,000 fatalities,

. Although the intensity of the epidemic has decreased, with the latest WHO report confirming 30 new confirmed cases in the week of April 5, the lowest since May 2014, challenges persist. In Liberia and Sierra Leone, the number of cases has decreased to the point where there are more treatment facilities than needed, leading to the decommissioning of some facilities. However, in Guinea, there are ongoing issues with unidentified Ebola cases and unsafe burials, indicating potential for new infections. This situation underscores the need for continued vigilance and improved surveillance to prevent future outbreaks.

The video of Officer Michael Slager shooting Walter Scott highlights the critical need for police body cameras. Such cameras not only ensure accountability but also potentially prevent tragic incidents

The deployment of body cameras, despite the costs associated with them, is justified by the value of human life and the need to restore public trust in law enforcement. Additionally, addressing underlying biases in the criminal justice system through stricter hiring standards, enhanced training, and broader social reforms is essential for long-term change. In the interim, body cameras serve as a crucial tool to mitigate the immediate risks posed by racial bias in policing.

Tomasz Lazar spent hours photographing and interviewing Polish survivors who were forcibly deported to Siberia as children during World War II. These individuals, now in their 80s, experienced significant trauma and were unable to share their stories publicly until after the fall of the Soviet Union. Lazar's portraits aim to document their struggles and remind future generations of the importance of learning from history. His work highlights the resilience of these individuals despite their painful pasts. Their stories should be preserved to ensure that such tragedies are not repeated.

The story highlights the harrowing experiences of Yazidi families, particularly Ahlam and her family, who were captured by ISIS and later managed to escape. Ahlam's family was taken from their home in Sinjar and moved from village to village, with her husband initially believing them dead. They were eventually able to reunite in Iraqi Kurdistan, but their ordeal was marked by fear and trauma, especially regarding the fate of the Yazidi women and girls who were taken as captives and subjected to horrific conditions.

Debra Lobo, a 55-year-old American citizen working as vice principal at Jinnah Medical and Dental College in Karachi, Pakistan, was shot in the right cheek and left arm while driving her daughters from school. The assailants, who fled on a motorcycle, left pamphlets in Urdu threatening to burn America. Lobo is unconscious but expected to survive and is receiving treatment at Aga Khan Hospital. The U.S. Consulate General in Karachi is in contact with Pakistani authorities to gather more information about the incident
Police are currently investigating the attack.

The family from Delaware, including Steve Esmond and his two teenage sons, fell ill after possibly being exposed to a pesticide called methyl bromide during their stay at the Sirenusa resort in St. John, U.S. Virgin Islands. The boys and their father are in critical condition, with the boys in a coma and the father unable to move or talk. Their mother, Theresa Devine, has been released from the hospital but is now in occupational therapy. The EPA is investigating the incident, and a criminal investigation has been initiated by the U.S. Department of Justice. The resort's rental agent, Sea Glass Vacations, stated that the unit directly below the family's was treated for pests, but their unit was not. Terminix, the pest control company involved, is cooperating with authorities and conducting an internal investigation.

The American Pharmacists Association has adopted a ban on its members participating in executions, aligning with its previous stances against the use of the term "drug" for chemicals used in lethal injection and against laws that mandate or prohibit pharmacist involvement in lethal injection cases. This decision was influenced by recent public scrutiny of lethal injection methods, including issues with medication quality and availability. The association emphasizes that such activities are inconsistent with the healthcare provider role of pharmacists.

The President noted that past efforts, like the Clean Air Act, have shown that addressing environmental issues can lead to significant health improvements and economic benefits. This approach aims to engage individuals and communities by focusing on personal health rather than abstract environmental concerns.

Cyclist Wang Pingan had his bike stolen just days before completing his 460-day cycling trip across China, but the Shenzhen police managed to recover it and return it to him. The bike had been sold twice after the theft but was eventually found thanks to surveillance cameras. Wang, who had vowed to walk if he couldn't get his bike back, was able to continue his journey to Hainan Island. His bike, which he considered a best friend, had accompanied him through over 29,200 kilometers, including some of the country's most remote areas.

Walmart's opposition to religious freedom laws in Arkansas highlights its

reflecting a broader shift in public opinion and business practices towards greater support for gay rights. This stance puts Walmart at odds with traditional Republican positions and suggests the company may serve as a bellwether for future political trends, particularly regarding issues affecting the middle class and workplace equality. A former Minnesota governor even urged the Republican Party to take note of Walmart's actions, indicating a potential need for the party to adapt its policies to better align with these changing views. This shift is partly driven by Walmart's diverse consumer base and an acknowledgment of the country's increasingly progressive stance on gay equality. The backlash against similar laws in Indiana also demonstrated widespread business support for nondiscrimination policies, with companies across the political spectrum voicing their opposition.

commemorates the resurrection of Jesus Christ, celebrated on the first Sunday following the first full moon after the vernal equinox, unless the full moon falls on a Sunday, in which case Easter is celebrated the following Sunday. This astronomical determination often aligns with lunar events, such as the blood moon, which appeared between Good Friday and Easter Sunday, coinciding with Passover. The holiday's date varies each year due to its reliance on lunar cycles rather than fixed dates. Easter's traditions, such as the Easter bunny and colored eggs, have pagan origins but were adopted into Christian celebrations, with the bunny possibly introduced to North America by German immigrants in the 1700s. For a fun and non-vinegary way to color eggs, using Kool-Aid is suggested, though it can stain. Happy Easter and Passover!

The Japanese government's top military official confirmed that the Air Self Defense Force (ASDF) has never encountered an extraterrestrial unidentified flying object. This response was given to flamboyant former wrestler-turned-lawmaker Antonio Inoki during a parliamentary session. Inoki, known for his wrestling career and various business ventures, had inquired about UFO sightings and the ASDF's response

Lauren Hill, who inspired many with her fight against brain cancer and her passion for basketball, passed away at the age of 19. Her story gained worldwide recognition when she was allowed to play her first college basketball game, raising $1.4 million for pediatric cancer research. Her legacy of positivity and determination continues to inspire people around the world.

Richie Benaud
the former Australia cricket captain and legendary broadcaster, died at the age of 84. He was known for his wit and insightful commentary, which resonated with cricket fans worldwide
Benaud was treated for skin cancer in his last year. His contributions to cricket were recognized with his induction into the Sport Australia Hall of Fame in 1985

As a player, he was a record-breaking leg-spinner and captain, leading Australia without losing a series in his 28 Tests as captain. His legacy as a broadcaster was marked by his distinctive voice and insightful commentary, making him a beloved figure in cricket.

NASA astrophysicist Amy Mainzer named an asteroid after Malala Yousafzai, the Pakistani education activist, in recognition of her contributions to promoting girls' education. The asteroid, designated 316201 Malala or 2010 ML48, is located in the asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter, orbiting the sun every five and a half years. Mainzer, who noted the scarcity of asteroids named after women, especially women of color, made this decision to honor Malala's resilience and advocacy. This gesture underscores the importance of supporting education for all, as it enhances human potential and addresses global challenges effectively.

An explosion outside the Bauchi Motor Park in Gombe, Nigeria, killed at least five people and injured more than a dozen others on Thursday evening. The attack occurred when a woman left an explosives-laden handbag near a bus, causing injuries including amputations and eye damage. No group has claimed responsibility, but Boko Haram is suspected due to their history of targeting soft spots like bus stations. This incident follows Nigeria's presidential election, which was won by Muhammadu Buhari, who plans to address Boko Haram upon taking office.

such as the Cartagena incident involving the Secret Service and the confrontational moment with Hugo Chavez. To succeed, Obama must articulate a clear vision for the region, focusing on democracy, free trade, and economic growth, while also addressing issues like human rights and corruption. His

The harassment ranges from touching and groping to more subtle forms like staring and catcalling
The author highlights the societal tendency to overlook these behaviors, which can lead women to limit their activities and choices. She emphasizes the importance of

about sexual harassment and acknowledges the challenges women face in doing so, including cultural barriers and fear of the legal system. Despite these challenges, she points to recent cases where women have successfully reported harassment, leading to significant outcomes. The author advocates for women to find the courage to speak out against harassment to empower themselves and future generations.

Nepal has issued 347 permits for this year, a slight increase from last year. The northern route from China has seen an increase in popularity, with 320 registered climbers, compared to 184 last year, due to concerns over safety and weather conditions on the Nepal side. Many Sherpas, despite being ready to return to the summit, face pressure from their families who are still affected by the memories of last year's tragedy.

The author argues that while diagnostic tests can be useful for acute medical issues, they may not benefit well individuals and could lead to unnecessary anxiety, procedures, and treatments. Canadian fighter jets conducted their first airstrike against ISIS in Syria, targeting a garrison near Raqqa, as part of the U.S.-led coalition's efforts to combat the extremist group. The operation was deemed successful, with the aircraft and crews safely returning to base. This marks an expansion of Canada's military involvement from Iraq to Syria, reflecting the government's commitment to addressing terrorism and promoting regional stability.

The Busby family welcomed the first set of female quintuplets in the world since 1969, born on April 8 in Houston. The Rademacher family also had a surprising birth, keeping the news of expecting twins a secret until the birth of Mary Ann Grace and Brianna Faith on March 11 in Pensacola, Florida. Savannah has been open about her relationship with Robert, expressing her desire to protect and love him, and appreciating the role he plays in her life, especially given her responsibilities as a guardian to her siblings. Robert, who has children of his own, seems to understand the importance of family stability. For now, Robert needs to finalize his divorce before any serious commitments can be considered.

Luna reveals Eric Forrester's diagnosis to Ridge Forrester, believing she is helping RJ by relieving him of the burden of keeping this secret. Ridge becomes upset with RJ for not sharing the information, while RJ feels betrayed by Luna's actions. Ridge advises RJ to forgive Luna for what she perceives as the right thing to do. The motives behind Luna's actions and whether RJ will forgive her remain to be seen in future episodes of The Bold and the Beautiful.

Cristina Michelle Husbands, a 48-year-old woman from Dallas, Texas, was sentenced to 64 months in prison for her role in a multi-state bank fraud scheme that resulted in the theft of hundreds of thousands of dollars from banks across several states. She was also ordered to pay $481,140 in restitution and will be under supervised release for five years following her prison term.

The proposal by Representative Eddies Morales to charge migrants $2,000 to enter the country is presented as a potential solution to manage the influx of migrants and generate revenue. However, whether it is a win-win situation for the country depends on various factors including the economic impact, ethical considerations, and the effectiveness of such a policy in addressing the root causes of migration. The idea aims to create a structured system for migrant labor, but its broader implications and acceptance would require further analysis and debate.

Jag Bains won the Power of Veto competition in Big Brother 25, giving him the chance to save one of the nominees from eviction. However, Jag has no intention of using this power to save either Blue Kim or America Lopez, with Blue being his primary target for eviction. With only four votes cast and Jag needing one more to ensure Blue's eviction, the outcome remains uncertain but leans towards Blue's exit. Additionally, Jag cannot compete in the next HOH competition, so Matt Klotz must win to protect Jag from the upcoming double eviction. Big Brother 25 continues to air on CBS and is available for streaming on Paramount Plus.

Brooke is blindsided by the news of Deacon's relationship with Sheila, as revealed by Hope and confirmed by Finn. She confronts Deacon, who admits to the affair, believing people can change. Brooke warns him he will lose everyone important to him if he doesn't end it. Later, Brooke informs Ridge about the situation, only to find out Ridge has known about it for a while but chose to keep it from her to avoid upsetting her. The question remains whether Brooke can convince Deacon to leave Sheila.

Kelly Clarkson has added new residency dates at the Bakkt Theater at Planet Hollywood Resort & Casino, including performances on New Year’s Eve and Superbowl weekend. Lionel Richie will continue his "King of Hearts" residency at Encore Theater at Wynn Las Vegas from June 26 to November 16, 2024. Both residencies are highly anticipated by fans and are expected to be highlights of Las Vegas's entertainment scene in 2024.

Whether automotive mechanics and technicians are entitled to overtime wages depends on four factors: where they work (auto dealer or repair shop), how they are paid (commission or not), how much they make (regular hourly rate and commissions), and the state in which they work. Automotive technicians in Alaska have the highest average pay at about $23.70 per hour or $49,400 per year. Those in aerospace products and parts manufacturing earn the highest hourly rate, averaging about $32 per hour or $66,300 per year.
Metoprolol succinate, also known as Toprol, is the long-acting form of the drug and can be taken once a day, providing a 24-hour effect in the bloodstream. Metoprolol tart
, known as Lopressor, is a shorter-acting form and typically needs to be taken at least twice a day. Succinate is derived from succinic acid and is an extended-release drug,
tartrate is derived from tartaric acid and is an immediate-release drug.
metoprolol tartrate may cause diarrhea and sleep issues such as insomnia or unusual dreams.

or deoxyribonucleic acid, is the hereditary material in humans and almost all other organisms. Each DNA molecule is composed of nucleotides, which include a phosphate group, a sugar group, and one of four nitrogen bases: adenine (A), thymine (T), guanine (G), and cytosine (C). These bases pair specifically—adenine with thymine, and guanine with cytosine—to form the rungs of the ladder-like double helix structure, with the sugar and phosphate molecules forming the vertical sidepieces. This structure allows DNA to replicate accurately, ensuring the genetic information is passed on correctly.

To prepare the pollock fillets, first preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Season the pollock fillets with paprika and black pepper. Place the fillets on a baking sheet sprayed with butter flavored cooking spray, and layer them with onion, green pepper, and tomato slices. Bake for about 15 minutes until the fish is cooked and flakes easily. After removing from the oven, place two slices of cheese on each fillet, return to the oven, and allow the cheese to melt for about 3 minutes.

To remove plastic from a dryer's interior, first run the dryer on a regular setting for 20 minutes to soften the plastic. Scrape the softened plastic off using a wooden or plastic spatula or paint scraper carefully. Apply 1 to 2 teaspoons of nail polish remover to a clean cloth and rub the area to dissolve the plastic. To create a pattern for the frustum of a cone with a base diameter of 2 inches, a top diameter of 0.25 inches, and a height of 7 inches, follow these steps:

1. Draw the full cone extending up to a point, which includes the top diameter of 0.25 inches and the height of 7 inches.
2. Calculate the slant height (w) using the Pythagorean theorem, where the height (h) is 7 inches and the radius difference between the base and the top is (2/2 - 0.25/2) = 0.875 inches.
3. Use the slant height to draw the sector of a circle that will form the frustum when rolled up.
4. Adjust the sector to account for the smaller top diameter.

The weighted average method of costing inventory calculates the average cost of all available inventory units and assigns this average cost to each unit sold. This approach ensures that the inventory cost reflects a value between the oldest and newest units purchased.
